steps.title: Find the slope from the table
steps.content: As $x$ increases by 1 unit (from $-11$ to $-10$ to $-9$ to $-8$), the function values decrease by 3 each time: $21 \to 18 \to 15 \to 12$. For a linear function, the slope is $m = \frac{\Delta f(x)}{\Delta x} = \frac{-3}{1} = -3$.

steps.title: Write the equation using point-slope form
steps.content:
Use the point $(-10, 18)$ from the table with slope $m = -3$:
$$f(x) = -3x + b$$
Substitute the point:
$$18 = -3(-10) + b$$
$$18 = 30 + b$$
$$b = -12$$
Therefore, $f(x) = -3x - 12$.

steps.title: Set f(x) = 0 to find the x-intercept
steps.content:
The $x$-intercept occurs where $f(x) = 0$:
$$0 = -3x - 12$$
$$3x = -12$$
$$x = -4$$
The $x$-intercept is at $(-4, 0)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
